findings.finding: 53 agent functions receive task_id as a parameter (used in the prompt text) but never pass task_id= into their run_agent_graph() call, which defaults it to "".
findings.evidence: accessibility_agent.py:102 uses task_id in an f-string; the run_agent_graph(...) call at lines 111-126 has no task_id= kwarg. Confirmed by direct read on 5 representative agents. Real task_id is available at the dispatch site: api/specialized_agents.py SpecializedAgentRequest.task_id (line 167), a required real DevTask.id.
findings.production_impact: Day 18 real-time activity-stream SSE events (push_thinking/push_tool_call/push_done, all gated on if task_id:) never fire for these 53 agents when dispatched via /api/specialized-agents/{name}/run(-sync). TaskStarted/TaskCompleted/TaskFailed/HealthUpdated fleet events record task_id="" instead of the real id, breaking correlation -- the same bug class the Days-0-18 gap-closure fixed internally in base_graph.py, recurring here caller-side across the majority of the fleet. DevTask status itself is unaffected (transition_task() called directly and correctly elsewhere).
findings.recommendation: Add task_id=task_id to the run_agent_graph(...) call in each of the 53 files, matching the pattern already correct in backend_dev.py/frontend_dev.py/coder.py/qa.py/reviewer.py.
findings.fix_note: Added task_id=str(task_id) (str() required -- run_agent_graph's task_id param is str-typed) to all 53 files; 52 via a scripted span-verified insertion, planner.py by hand. Re-verified via fresh AST pass: 0/53 missing.

findings.location: per-agent model field for sonnet-tier (62) and opus-tier (9) entries
findings.finding: agent_models.json (authoritative over any agent-passed fallback model, per model_router.py) pins 62 agents to claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and 9 to claude-opus-4-20250514, while config.py's own model_coder default is claude-sonnet-5 -- a different, newer identifier. Only the 2 haiku-tier entries match config.py's defaults.
findings.evidence: agent_models.json:9 DEFAULT model=claude-sonnet-4-20250514, repeated at 62 sonnet entries; config.py:32 model_coder default=claude-sonnet-5. File's own _comment says "all 68 Gridiron agents" -- stale (73 entries, 72 real agents).
findings.production_impact: 71 of 72 agents silently run an older model generation than config.py's documented default implies, since model_router.py always overrides the passed-in fallback. Not a crash, but a real drift between two config sources CLAUDE.md's own model-in-config rule exists to prevent.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile agent_models.json's model strings with config.py's current defaults, or derive tier->model from Settings at load time instead of maintaining two files by hand.
findings.fix_note: Bulk-replaced claude-sonnet-4-20250514 -> claude-sonnet-5 (63 entries) and claude-opus-4-20250514 -> claude-opus-4-8 (9 entries); haiku entries were already correct. Updated stale _comment (68->72 agents).

findings.finding: No anthropic.Anthropic(...) client construction or .messages.create(...) call anywhere in base_graph.py passes an explicit timeout=. Relies entirely on the SDK's undocumented built-in default.
findings.evidence: grep -n "timeout" app/agents/base_graph.py app/agents/base.py app/config.py returns zero matches.
findings.production_impact: A hung LLM connection could block an asyncio.to_thread() worker indefinitely with no project-level control to bound it -- matches this audit's own stated concern verbatim.
findings.recommendation: Add a config field (e.g. LLM_CALL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) and pass timeout=settings.llm_call_timeout_seconds to every Anthropic client construction in base_graph.py.
findings.fix_note: Added llm_call_timeout_seconds (default 300.0) to config.py. Added shared _make_client() helper in base_graph.py (all 4 sites now route through it) and updated base.py's existing _make_client() the same way.

findings.finding: Every submit_* tool handler does result.update(inp) -- a raw, unvalidated dict merge of the LLM's tool-call arguments. No Pydantic model validates inp against the tool's declared input_schema before it is trusted.
findings.evidence: grep -n "\.update(inp)" app/agents/tools.py matches at minimum 7 sites across QA, review, research, docs, bug-fix, security, and architecture-review handlers.
findings.production_impact: Malformed-but-silently-defaulted output risk for non-boolean fields (summaries, findings, recommendations) -- downstream code reads with .get(key, default), masking gaps rather than surfacing them. The boolean verification contract (state["verification"] override) is unaffected by this finding.
findings.recommendation: Define a Pydantic model per submit_* tool and validate inp through a shared helper before merging into result.
findings.fix_note: Implemented differently than originally recommended, for good reason: instead of one Pydantic model per tool (dozens, real scope bloat), added centralized jsonschema validation in base_graph.py's execute_tools() -- the one chokepoint every submit_* call already passes through -- reusing each tool's existing input_schema. jsonschema==4.26.0 installed and pinned in requirements.txt. A mismatch is non-fatal (logged + result["_validation_warning"]). Covers all ~72 agents uniformly (tools.py handlers and per-agent inline handlers alike). Live-verified: malformed submit flagged without crashing the run; well-formed submit unaffected.

fix_verification.pytest: 2758 passed, 0 failed, 55 skipped, 17 deselected (unchanged from pre-fix baseline)
fix_verification.mypy_strict: 0 errors, 176 source files (caught a real int/str type bug in the AGENT-02-001 fix itself, corrected before this passed clean)

not_found: Capability tag collisions -- 0 found across all 72 real agents, re-confirmed fresh
Orphaned/unreachable agents -- 0 found; full real-caller dispatch map traced for all 72
Tool-scope violations on read-only-named agents -- 0 found among 44 read-only-tool-scope agents checked
Dead enforce_in_result keys (not backed by a set_by value) -- 0 found across all 72
_register() defined but not called at top level -- 0 found
Missing role files -- 0 genuinely missing (chat_agent initially flagged by automated scan was a false positive: it loads roles/chat.md under a different filename, verified working)

Cross-provider fallback on Anthropic call failure -- confirmed NOT present (retry-same-provider only via the outer exception handler; the only cross-provider path is the separate USE_GROQ=true bypass, not a runtime fallback)
Retry-state leak across tasks/runs -- confirmed clean; AgentRunState constructed fresh per run_agent_graph() call
